entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: An important macronutrient and the most abundant cation in higher plants playing a key role in various cellular processes

entities.name: TRANSPORTER PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins that mediate the accumulation of potassium from soil and its distribution throughout diverse plant tissues

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: Oryza sativa L., a model plant for cereals with a completed genome sequencing project

entities.name: ARABIDOPSIS
entities.description: A model plant used for studying K+ transporters, though phylogenetically distant from cereals

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of an organism's genome, used to inventory K+ transporter proteins in rice

entities.name: PHYLOGENETIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: The study of evolutionary relationships among biological entities, used to analyze K+ transporters in rice

entities.name: HAK FAMILY
entities.description: A family of potassium transporter genes in rice represented by 26 genes

entities.name: K+ CHANNEL FAMILY
entities.description: A family of potassium transporter genes in rice containing 14 genes

entities.name: 1P/6TM SHAKER-TYPE
entities.description: A subfamily of the K+ channel family in rice consisting of 11 genes

entities.name: 2P/4TM ORK-TYPE
entities.description: A subfamily of the K+ channel family in rice consisting of 3 genes

entities.name: TRK FAMILY
entities.description: A family of potassium transporter genes in rice represented by 9 genes

entities.name: K+/H+ ANTIPORTER FAMILY
entities.description: A family of potassium transporter genes in rice represented by a single gene

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop used for comparative analysis of K+ channels

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop used for comparative analysis of K+ channels

entities.name: COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of comparing biological features across different species, used to compare K+ channels in rice, Arabidopsis, wheat, and maize

relationships.description: Rice requires potassium for cellular processes
Rice genome encodes membrane transport proteins
Used to inventory K+ transporter proteins in rice
Used to analyze K+ transporters in rice
Used to identify the HAK family as a distinct branch
Used to identify the K+ channel family
Used to identify the Trk family
Used to identify the K+/H+ antiporter family
Used to compare rice K+ channels with other species
Used to compare rice K+ channels with Arabidopsis
Used to compare rice K+ channels with wheat
Used to compare rice K+ channels with maize
